creator: hselasky@c2i.net
text:
Hi,

The patch looks like a compile fix only ...

Some thoughts.

1) LibUSB0.1 should really not need to be installed any more on FreeBSD-8 
current except for the CPP part which I think is not used?

2) LibUSB0.1 port can check if libusb20 is present and install the 
documentation, if any. Also the LibUSB0.1 port can compile the libusb0.1 
tests.


Here are some patches which do not work 100% because I'm not a autoconf 
expert, but it might give some clues:


diff -u -r libusb-0.1.12.orig/Makefile.am libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am
--- libusb-0.1.12.orig/Makefile.am      Mon Mar  9 09:00:25 2009
+++ libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am   Mon Mar  9 11:53:34 2009
@@ -23,6 +23,10 @@

 pkgconfig_DATA = libusb.pc

+if LIBUSB20_API
+OS_SUPPORT =
+LDADDS =
+else
 if LINUX_API
 OS_SUPPORT = linux.c linux.h
 else
@@ -40,12 +44,17 @@
 endif
 endif
 endif
+endif

 AM_CFLAGS += $(AM_CFLAGS_EXT)

 AM_CPPFLAGS = -I.
 AM_CXXFLAGS =

+if LIBUSB20_API
+libusb_la_SOURCES = dummy.c
+libusbpp_la_SOURCES = usbpp.cpp usbpp.h
+else
 libusb_la_SOURCES = usb.c usbi.h error.c error.h descriptors.c $(OS_SUPPORT)
 libusbpp_la_SOURCES = usbpp.cpp usbpp.h

@@ -54,6 +63,8 @@
 # Do not add usb.h to *_SOURCES, regardless of dependencies.
 # It would be added to the dist tarball, which it shouldn't be.
 nodist_include_HEADERS = usb.h
+endif
+
 include_HEADERS = usbpp.h

 libusb_la_LDFLAGS = \



diff -u -r libusb-0.1.12.orig/configure.in libusb-0.1.12/configure.in
--- libusb-0.1.12.orig/configure.in     Mon Mar  9 09:00:25 2009
+++ libusb-0.1.12/configure.in  Mon Mar  9 12:13:35 2009
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@
 #undef LINUX_API
 #undef BSD_API
 #undef DARWIN_API
+#undef LIBUSB20_API

 #undef HAVE_OLD_DEV_USB_USB_H

@@ -128,13 +129,12 @@
 AC_SUBST(DARWIN_API)
 AC_SUBST(LINUX_API)
 AC_SUBST(BSD_API)
+AC_SUBST(OSLIBS)
 
 AM_CONDITIONAL(LINUX_API, test "$os_support" = "linux")
 AM_CONDITIONAL(BSD_API, test "$os_support" = "bsd")
 AM_CONDITIONAL(DARWIN_API, test "$os_support" = "darwin")

-AC_SUBST(OSLIBS)
-
 if test `expr $LIBUSB_MINOR_VERSION \% 2` = 1 ; then
   debug_default=yes
 else
@@ -189,6 +189,21 @@
 # Check for some functions
 AC_CHECK_FUNCS(memmove)
 
+# Check for LibUSB20 (FreeBSD-8)
+AC_CHECK_LIB([usb20],[usb_open])
+
+if test $ac_cv_lib_usb20_usb_open = yes; then
+AC_DEFINE(LIBUSB20_API, 1)
+LIBUSB20_API=1
+else
+AC_DEFINE(LIBUSB20_API, 0)
+LIBUSB20_API=0
+fi
+
+AM_CONDITIONAL(LIBUSB20_API, test $LIBUSB20_API = "1")
+  
+AC_SUBST(LIBUSB20_API)
+
 if test "$os_support" = "bsd"; then
   AC_MSG_CHECKING(if dev/usb/usb.h uses new naming convention)
   AC_COMPILE_IFELSE([AC_LANG_PROGRAM([[#include <dev/usb/usb.h>]], [[
@@ -196,6 +211,7 @@
      ]])],[AC_MSG_RESULT(yes)],[AC_MSG_RESULT(no)
     AC_DEFINE_UNQUOTED(HAVE_OLD_DEV_USB_USB_H)])
 fi
+

 #pkg-config: pkg-config is a tool to simplify checks for libraries and 
versions.
 pkgconfig_msg="no (http://www.freedesktop.org/software/pkgconfig)"




--HPS
